message:
Last month our power went out for about six hours, and I genuinely expected my kids to lose their minds. Instead, we ended up playing board games by flashlight, telling stories, and my oldest actually *asked* to do this again sometime.

It sounds cheesy, but it hit me that we'd accidentally created this cozy, phone-free bubble—no notifications, no background hum of devices. We were just... present.

Of course, I also made sure our power banks were charged beforehand (so we could check on things if needed) and kept our fridge closed like it was Fort Knox. The boredom part was a happy accident, but the *preparedness* part? That let me actually relax and enjoy it.

Honestly, having a few basics ready—good flashlights, a charged power bank, some shelf-stable snacks—takes so much of the stress out of these moments. Leaves room for the unexpected good stuff.

What's the most surprisingly fun thing that's happened to your family during a power hiccup?
